title: Bitter Creamy Pepper Stir-fry
description: A lazy Sunday Australian dish featuring peppers that are both bitter and creamy, cooked to perfection in a low-calorie-friendly way.

ingredients.name: Bell Peppers
ingredients.notes: Includes both sweet and spicy varieties

ingredients.name: Parsley or Chives for Garnish (optional)
ingredients.notes: For presentation, not required for eating

instructions: Wash and thinly slice the bell peppers.
Mince the garlic cloves and set aside.
In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic and cook for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add sliced bell peppers to the skillet and stir-fry for about 5 minutes until they start to soften and slightly char on the edges.
Pour in the low-sodium chicken broth or water, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Bring it to a simmer and let cook for an additional minute to reduce slightly.
Add heavy cream, salt, pepper, and freshly squeezed lemon juice to the skillet. Stir well until fully combined.
Reduce heat to low and cover the skillet with a lid to help seal in moisture. Cook on low for about 10 minutes or until the peppers are tender but still have a slight crunch.
Remove from heat and serve hot, garnished with optional parsley or chives if desired.
